title: Pithecellobium filipes
taxonomy: Pithecellobium filipes is classified under the kingdom Plantae, specifically within the family Fabaceae, commonly known as legumes. This classification is due to its characteristic pea-like flowers and seed pods. Within the Fabaceae family, Pithecellobium filipes belongs to the subfamily Faboideae. Further classification places it within the tribe Ingeae, a group of tropical legumes. The species itself is classified under the genus Pithecellobium, a group of flowering plants native to the Americas.

intro: Pithecellobium filipes, also known as the "Cochineal tree," is a plant species in the legume family, characterized by its striking red flowers and edible seeds. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects, making it beneficial for treating wounds, skin conditions, and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of P. filipes include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which have been shown to possess antimicrobial and anticancer properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as topical creams and ointments, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
